info.title: Default module
info.description:
Cloudflare Worker API for handling file and image uploads for Salla merchants.

## Features
- Unified endpoint for both file and image uploads
- Automatic image optimization with smart aspect ratio preservation
- Images are resized to fit within maximum dimensions while maintaining aspect ratio
- Support for multiple output formats (JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF)
- Built on Cloudflare R2 storage with global CDN distribution
- Automatic MIME type validation and file extension checks
- Support for multiple file categories (orders, payments, products, etc.)
- Private storage option for digital products

## Image Processing
- All uploaded images are automatically optimized
- Default settings: 1920×1080 max dimensions, JPEG format, 90% quality
- Width and height parameters act as maximum bounds (aspect ratio preserved)
- Supports JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, and AVIF input formats
- Optimized images must be under 512KB

## Base URL
The API is deployed on Cloudflare Workers and uses the base path `/admin/v2/files/uploader`.

## Authentication
All endpoints require a valid merchant authentication token passed via the Authorization header.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to update the status of a specific order by passing the `order_id` as a path parameter. 


:::tip[Note]
To update an order status, you may use one of the following:
- `slug`: to update an order with predefined Salla statuses.
- `status_id`: to update the Custom Order Sub-status.
:::
<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`orders.read_write` - Orders Read & Write
</Accordion>

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unique identification number assigend to an order. Get a list of Order IDs from [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394146).

status_id.description: A unique identifier associated with a specific order status.

slug.description: A user-friendly and URL-friendly text string associated with a specific order status.

note.description: A written comment or message submitted to request information or updates regarding the status of the order.

restore_items.description: A boolean value indicating whether items should be returned to inventory if the status allows restoration, which can be set from store settings

data.message: The entity has been updated successfully

error.message: The access token should have access to one of those scopes: orders.read_write

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update a custom order status by passing the `status_id` as a path parameter.

:::tip[Note]
Use available parameters to send as a body request.
:::

:::danger[Important]
All variables in the Update Custom Order Status body request are optional. At least one of the variables **must** be in the body request payload. Otherwise, an error will be shown if you send an empty body request payload.
:::

`orders.read_write` - Orders Read & Write

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to restore a single product that is associated with the Merchant's store by passing the `id` of the product as a path parameter.

:::warning[Note]
In case you did not pass a Product ID, the endpoint will restore all deleted products associated with the Merchant's store.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`products.read_write` - Products Read & Write
</Accordion>

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Product ID you want to restore. To restore all deleted products, do not send a Product ID as a body parameter.

post.description:
Products can be created within your store at Salla by providing the required data in this endpoint.

:::tip[Note]
You can list available categories from [List Categories](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394207/) endpoint.
:::


:::caution[Alert]
- Created products status will be set to `hidden` till attaching one image at least.
- Created products status will be set to`out` till adding a value in quantity parameter.
- Created products type cannot be changed after a product is created.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`products.read_write`- Products Read & Write
</Accordion>

name.description: A Product Name is a succinct label for a specific item, aiding in easy identification and categorization within a product listing.

price.description: Product price is the specified cost assigned to a particular item, determining its value in a commercial transaction.

status.description: Product status refers to the current condition or state of availability of a particular item within a product catalog or inventory. Available values 'hidden','sale','out'.

product_type.description: Product type categorizes items based on shared attributes or characteristics, aiding in effective organization and search within a product inventory. Available values are, 'product', 'service', 'group_products', 'codes', 'digital', 'food', and 'donating'

quantity.description: Product quantity indicates the numerical count of a specific item available in inventory, representing its availability for sale or distribution.

location.description: The specific place or site where a reservation or booking is made.

type.description: Booking Type. Required if `booking_details != null`

session_duration.description: The length of time that a particular session. Required if `type = date_`

session_gap.description: The period of time between two consecutive sessions or events, indicating the duration between the end of one session and the start of the next.. Required if `type = date_time`

session_count.description: Booking Sessions Count, which is the number of sessions allowed for one customer . Required if `booking_details != null`

time_strict_value.description: A timeframe where it is not applicable to make reservations.

time_strict_type.description: The restriction on making bookings within a specific timeframe is measured in minutes, hours, or days.

day.description: Days which are available for booking.

from.description: The starting time of the of available booking slot in 24 hours format. Required if `booking_details.type = date_time`

to.description: The ending time of the of available booking slot in 24 hours format. Required if `booking_details.type = date_time`

overrides.description: Overwrite availability for a particular day, on a particular date

unlimited_quantity.description: Option to indicate the product is of unlimited quantity.

description.description: A product description provides detailed information about an item, helping customers understand its features, benefits, and intended use.

categories.description: A list of categories ID's, that will be associated with the product.

cost_price.description: Product cost price, the amount a business pays to acquire or manufacture a product before any additional expenses, such as overhead or markup, are applied.

sale_end.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to expire.

sale_start.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to start.

require_shipping.description: Require shipping indicates whether the product necessitates a physical delivery process or can be provided digitally without shipping.

maximum_quantity_per_order.description: Maximum quantity per order for the customer or the maximum quantity for the product.

weight.description: The weight of the product represented in numerical form if applicable.

videos.description: As well as a single video as mentioned above, It also can be list of videos

sku.description: Sku of the product.

gtin.description:
Global Trade Item Number. If `product_type` is set to any of the following:  `product`, `group_products`, `codes`, `digital`, `donating`
then value can be set. Otherwise, it can be set to `null`

calories.description: Calories Value. If `product_type` is set to`food`, value can bet set. Otherwise, it can be set to `null`

hide_quantity.description: weather to hide the quantity or not.

show_in_web.description: Whether or not to change the product's status to `hidden` if set to `true` or `sale` if set to `false`

enable_upload_image.description: To enable upload image for the product when the customer add it to cart.

enable_note.description: To enable add note for the product when customer add it to cart.

pinned.description: if you want pinned the product in categories page.

active_advance.description: if you want to activate product advanced option.

subtitle.description: Subtitle of the product will display under product name in pages.

promotion_title.description: A label to display in the main image.

promotion_subtitle.description: A sublabel to display in the main image.

metadata_title.description: The page title for product will display in search engine results.

metadata_description.description: The page description for product will display in search engine results.

metadata_url.description: The page url for product will display in search engine results.

scope_id.description: Refers to the id of the scope/market, as of now you can get it from merchant dashboard.

price.description: The price of the product.

cost_price.description: Product cost price, the amount a business pays to acquire or manufacture a product before any additional expenses, such as overhead or markup, are applied

sale_price.description: The sale price of the product.

sale_end.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to expire.

sale_start.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to start.

scoped_prices.description: Will contain the prices details that related to the store markets/scopes

tags.description: The tag ids, check tags sections for more details.

consisted_products.description: The Product is under a Group of Products

id.description: Consisted Product ID

quantity.description: Consisted Product Quantity

default.description: Whether or not the image is the default one for the product

sort.description: Image's position in a list of other images within the product starting from the sort number 1.

images.description: We overide the currently attached images and replace them with the newly added ones for this product.

channels.description: The product to appear in specific channels

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to list all available products related to your store. 

:::check[Simplifed, Minimal Response]
If you want to receive a simple, data-reduced response when consuming the List Products endpoint, you can use the query parameter `format=light`
:::

:::note[Note]
This endpoint allows you to filter the list of available products by matching the following parameters:
- keyword
- status
- category 
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`products.read`- Products Read Only
</Accordion>

name.description: The designated title or label assigned to a particular product. 🌐 [Support multi-language](https://docs.salla.dev/421122m0)

price.description: The price of the product.

status.description: The current condition or state of a product, indicating whether it is available for sale, out of stock, hidden, deleted. List of Product Statuses can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394150).

quantity.description: Quantity of the product. Will be skippped if unlimted_quantity=true

location.description: The specific place or venue where a booking or reservation is made, such as a hotel, restaurant, event venue, or transportation service.

type.description: Refers to the category or classification of a reservation or booking, indicating the nature or purpose of the booking. Required if `booking_details != null`

session_duration.description: Booking duration is mandatory when the booking type is set as `date_time`. Required if `type = date_time`

session_gap.description: Booking session gap is necessary when the booking type is `date_time`, and it specifies the amount of time between consecutive booking sessions or appointments. Required if `type = date_time`

session_count.description: Booking Sessions Count, which is the number of sessions allowed for one customer. Required if `booking_details != null`

time_strict_value.description: A timeframe where it is not applicable to make reservations.

time_strict_type.description: The restriction on making bookings within a specific timeframe is measured in minutes, hours, or days.

day.description: Days which are available for booking.

from.description: The starting time of the available booking slot in 24-hour format. Required if `booking_details.type = date_time`.

to.description: The ending time of the available booking slot in 24-hour format. Required if `booking_details.type = date_time`.

overrides.description: Overwrite availability for a particular day, on a particular date

day.description: The value of specific date that has been overridden in a booking

unlimited_quantity.description: Option to indicate the product is of unlimited quantity.

description.description: A product description provides detailed information about an item, helping customers understand its features, benefits, and intended use. 🌐 [Support multi-language](docs/1.%20Introduction/1.6.Multi-Language-Support.md)

categories.description: A list of categories IDs, that will be associate with the product. List of Categories ID can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394207).

sale_price.description: The sale price of the product.

cost_price.description: Product cost price, the amount a business pays to acquire or manufacture a product before any additional expenses, such as overhead or markup, are applied

sale_end.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to expire.

sale_start.description: The date when a promotional sale or discount on a product is scheduled to start.

require_shipping.description: determent if the product require shipping or not.

maximum_quantity_per_order.description: Maximum product quantity for each order.

weight.description: The weight of the product represented in numerical form if applicable.

weight_type.description: The classification or unit of measurement used to specify the weight of an object or item, such as kilograms (kg), pounds (lb), ounces (oz), or grams (g).

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit is a unique alphanumeric code used to identify and manage specific products within a company's inventory. The Product SKU. List of Product SKU can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394168).

mpn.description: Manufacturer Part Number, a unique identifier assigned by a manufacturer to a specific product or component, which helps distinguish it from other similar products and facilitates inventory management, product tracking, and ordering processes.

gtin.description: "Global Trade Item Number" (GTIN), a unique and standardized identifier used to uniquely represent products, items, or services in the global marketplace, to enable efficient tracking and management across supply chains and retail sectors.

with_tax.description: The option to indicate that the product price includes tax or not.

hide_quantity.description: The option to hide product quantity.

show_in_web.description: The option  change the product's status to `hidden` if set to `true` or `sale` if set to `false`

channels.description: The product to appear in specific channels

enable_upload_image.description: To enable upload image for the product when the customer add it to cart.

enable_note.description: To enable add note for the product when customer add it to cart.

pinned.description: The option to enable pinning product in categories page.

active_advance.description: Option to activate advance product details.

subtitle.description: Subtitle of the product will display under product name in pages.

promotion_title.description: A label to display in the main image. 🌐 [Support multi-language](doc-421122)

promotion_subtitle.description: A sublabel to display in the main image. 🌐 [Support multi-language](doc-421122)

metadata_title.description: Product de SEO Metadata Title which is a concise label used to optimize search engine results and enhance the visibility of a product details page. 🌐 [Support multi-language](doc-421122)

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to list all orders related to your store.

:::danger[Breaking Change Notice]
Effective 18th February 2026, the `groups` array under the `customer` object will be deprecated. Alternatively, you can use the [Customer Details Endpoint](https://docs.salla.dev/5394122e0) to get the groups assigned to a specific customer.
:::

:::tip[Note]
• For old apps (expanded query parameter), the `shipping_details_id` inside the `shipping` object response matches the `id` returned by the [shipping estimated rate endpoint](https://docs.salla.dev/6899590e0).
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`orders.read` - Orders Read Only
</Accordion>

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to return the details for a specific order by passing the `order_id` as a path parameter. 

:::tip[Note]
• You can able to send the `reference_id` as a *query parameter* to get order details. Check the [List Orders Endpoint](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394146/?nav=1) for more.
• If you send the query parameter `format` and set it equal to `light`, you will receive the order details without Shipments, Items, Pickup Branch, and Customer Groups objects.
• For old apps (expanded query parameter), the `shipping_details_id` inside the `shipping` object response matches the `id` returned by the [shipping estimated rate endpoint](https://docs.salla.dev/6899590e0).
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`orders.read` - Orders Read Only
</Accordion>

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update a specific order by passing the `order_id` as a path parameter. 

:::tip[Tip]

- For a [darfted order](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5750257?nav=1), you have no restrictions to update any field.
- You can update the order based on the following five details:

|Object|Description|
| -- | -- |
| Customer | It can be modified if there's no associated `customer` or if the customer is  `guest`. |
| Receiver | It can be modified if the order status isn't marked as `completed`. |
| Shipping | It can be modified if the order status isn't marked as `completed`. |
| Payments | It can be modified if the order status is set to `pending`. |
| Coupon | It can be modified if the order status isn't marked as `completed`. |
:::

:::danger[Breaking Change Notice]
<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Introducing National Address" icon="material-two-tone-signpost">
    **Effective January 5th, 2026**, the [National Address](https://splonline.com.sa/en/national-address-1/) details are **mandatory** with the `ship_to` object as follows:
    - `country`
    - `city`
    - `address_line`
    - `street_number`
    - `block`
    - `short_address`
    - `building_number`
    - `additional_number`
    - `postal_code`
    - `geo_coordinates`
  </Card>

  <Card title="Deprecated variables" icon="material-two-tone-do_not_disturb">
  - The variables `data.checkout_url` and `data.rating_link` will be *deprecated*
by **May 25, 2025**. Moving forward, they will be accessible under the `urls`
object as `data.urls.checkout` and `data.urls.rating`.
  - The variables, `items.codes` and `items.files`, are deprecated. We recommend
using instead the `data.urls.digital_content` variable.
  - The extended response is deprecated for all apps created after **15th of August,
2024.** 
  </Card>
</CardGroup>


:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`orders.read_write` - Orders Read & Write
</Accordion>

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create external orders, that happened out of Salla platform.

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`orders.read_write` - Orders Read & Write
</Accordion>

external_order_id.description: Unique identifier for the order from the external system. Must be unique - duplicate IDs will be rejected.

date.description: Order creation date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'). Used for backdating imported orders.

source.description: Supported order sources: shopify, woocommerce, wix, trendyol, amazon, prestashop, magento, sitecore, zid, other (default).

status_name.description: Name of the order status (e.g., 'completed', 'pending'). Can be a native status or custom status name.

currency.description: Currency code for the order (e.g., 'SAR', 'USD'). Used for currency exchange calculations.

exchange_currency_rate.description: Exchange rate for the order currency. If not provided, the system will automatically calculate the rate based on current exchange rates.

subtotal.description: Order subtotal amount before tax, shipping, and discounts.

tax.description: Tax information for the entire order.

rate.description: Tax rate percentage (e.g., 15 for 15%). If not provided but value is set, rate is calculated automatically.

value.description: Absolute tax amount in the order currency.

id.description: Existing customer ID in the Salla system. Required to associate order with a customer.Required if not guest.

name.description: Customer Name, in case customer does not exist.

email.description: Customer Email, in case customer does not exist.

is_guest.description: Whether the customer is a guest (not registered).

products.description: List of products/items in the order.

name.description: Product name/title to display in the order.

price.description: Unit price per item in the order currency.

cost_price.description: Cost price for profit margin calculations.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit code for inventory tracking.

quantity.description: Number of units ordered. Defaults to 1 if not provided.

weight_type.description: Unit of weight measurement (e.g., 'kg', 'g', 'lb'). Uses store default if not specified.

require_shipping.description: Whether this product requires physical shipping. Defaults to true.

product_type.description: Type of product (e.g., 'product', 'digital', 'codes'). Affects shipping and fulfillment.

mpn.description: Manufacturer Part Number for product identification.

gtin.description: Global Trade Item Number (barcode) such as UPC, EAN, or ISBN.

product_discount.description: Discount amount applied to this specific product.

thumbnail.description: URL of the product thumbnail image.

tax.description: Tax information for this specific product.

rate.description: Tax rate percentage for this product. Auto-calculated if only value is provided.

value.description: Absolute tax amount for this product.

discounts.description: Discount/coupon information applied to the order.

code.description: External reference code for the discount/coupon.

shipping_amount.description: Discount amount applied to shipping. Must equal shipping_cost for free shipping.

status.description: Payment status. Allowed values: 'paid' or 'pending_payment'.

method.description: "Payment method used. Only 'cod' (Cash on Delivery) or 'external_epayment' are accepted. Required when status is 'paid'."

gateway.description: Custom payment gateway information for tracking purposes.

label.description: Display name/label for the custom payment gateway.

reference_id.description: Transaction reference ID from the payment gateway.

amount.description: COD fee amount to be collected on delivery.

receiver.description: Shipping recipient information (person receiving the order).

name.description: Full name of the receiver. Must be 4-200 characters.

phone.description: Phone number of the receiver (validated against country_code).

country_code.description: ISO country code for phone validation (e.g., 'SA', 'AE').

email.description: Email address of the receiver for notifications.

notify.description: Whether to send order notifications to the receiver (0 or 1).

delivery_method.description: Delivery method for the order. Values: 'shipping' (home delivery) or 'pickup' (store pickup).

courier_id.description: ID of the shipping company/courier. Must be a valid enabled courier for the store.

shipping_cost.description: Shipping cost amount. Set to 0 for free shipping.

country.description: Country ID from Salla's country list. Required if shippable.

city.description: City ID from Salla's city list. Required if shippable.

district.description: District ID from Salla's district list.

street_number.description: Street number of the delivery address.

postal_code.description: Postal/ZIP code for the delivery address.

geo_coordinates.description: Geographic coordinates for precise delivery location.

branch_id.description: Store branch ID for pickup orders or to specify the shipping origin branch.

ship_to.address_line: building

Advance configurations are possible using the available query parameters.

`exports.read`- Exports Read Only

parameters.name: filter_orders

parameters.description: Provide the `template_id` if the store has [`order-export-templates`](https://help.salla.sa/article/1765308414) feature enabled on the store. Query the list of templates from [this endpoint](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5593165). Default export template_id is set to `-1`

parameters.name: format

parameters.description: Filter from date. Required if the query parameter `filter_orders` is set to `dates`

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new option for a specific product by passing the `product` as a path parameter. 


:::tip[Note]
For the `product.type` variable set to `product`, creating a new product option will generate a new variant for this option.
:::

:::warning[Important]
If you're creating a new physical product with variants, the price must be set after creating options. call the [update product SKU](https://docs.salla.dev/5394204e0) to set the price for each variant.  Including the price in this case will result on a `422` validation error.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`products.read_write`- Products Read & Write
</Accordion>

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update variant details by passing the `variant` as a path parameter. 


:::caution[Important]
- All variables in the update variant body request are optional.
- Updating one variable at a time is possible, but at least one of the variables must be in the body request payload. Otherwise, an error will be shown if you send an empty body request payload.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`products.read_write`- Products Read & Write
</Accordion>

parameters.name: variant
parameters.description: Unique identification number assigned to a Product SKU Variant . List of Product SKU IDscan be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394168).

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit is a unique alphanumeric code used to identify and manage specific product variant within a company's inventory system.

barcode.description: The barcode value of product variant.

price.description: The price of the product variant.

sale_price.description: The sale price of the product variant. Example: 90.50

cost_price.description: The original or purchase price at which a product variant or item was acquired by a business or individual, excluding any additional expenses or markups.

stock_quantity.description: Amount of the product variant total stock quantity for the default branch, it well ignored if the `quantities` has been used in the body

gtin.description: Global Trade Item Number, a unique and standardized identifier used to uniquely represent products, items, or services in the global marketplace, to enable efficient tracking and management across supply chains and retail sector. If `product_type` is set to any of the following: `product`, `group_products`, `codes`, `digital`, `donating` then value can be set. Otherwise, it can be set to `null`

notify_low.description: Sets a threshold value to trigger notifications when inventory falls below.

branch.description: The branch id where you need to update the quantity

quantity.description: The new quantity

reason_id.description: The reason ID of the quantity change

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create Customers within your store at Salla by providing the required data in this endpoint. 

:::tip[Note]
- You may need the [Countries List](https://docs.salla.dev/endpoint-5394228) endpoint to get the mobile and country codes.
- Also, you may use the [Customer Groups list](https://docs.salla.dev/endpoint-5394129) to add the group's IDs for the new customer.
- Customer's email and mobile number are **unique**.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`customers.read_write`- Customers Read & Write
</Accordion>

get.description:
This endpoint lets you list all customers associated with your store and filter them using a keyword. It retrieves customers whose `"mobile number"`, `"email"`, or `"name"` match the keyword you provide.

:::info[]
The rate limit for the customers endpoints is **`500`** request per **`10`** minutes. For more details, please visit the [Rate Limiting](https://docs.salla.dev/421125m0) & [change log](https://docs.salla.dev/421127m0#291---2024-04-16) documentation page.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`customers.read`- Customers Read Only
</Accordion>

parameters.description: The Pagination page number

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description:
- `customer.mobile` | 966511804534
- `customer.name` | Del John
- `shipping_number` | Find a specific shipment by its ID from [here](api-5394160)
- `reference_id` | 613398835
- `tag_name` | Find a specific tag by its name from [here](api-5394154)

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a customer group by providing a required group name, along with conditions *(shared traits among members)* and the features that will apply to the group.

:::tip[Note]

The **type** variable may be one of the following options:

| |
| - |
| `"total_sales"` |
| `"total_orders"` |
| `"store_rating"` |
| `"doesnt_have_orders"` |


And the **symbols** may be one of the following options:

| |
| - |
| `>` |
| `<` |
| `between` |

:::

:::info[Information]
To add the payment methods you will need to get the payment methods list from the [Available Payment Methods](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394164) endpoint
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new category and return the category ID and its details.
The products inside the category are sorted independently. 

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`categories.read_write`- Categories Read & Write
</Accordion>

name.description: The title or label assigned to a specific grouping or classification of items, products, or content, often used to organize and categorize them for easier navigation or identification. 🌐 [Support multi-language](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421122)

status.description: The status of the category, whether or not it is `active` or `hidden`

metadata_title.description: Category SEO Metadata Title which is a concise label used to optimize search engine results and enhance the visibility of a category page.

metadata_description.description: A succinct summary crafted to enhance search engine optimization and spotlight a brand's attributes within a category.

metadata_url.description: Metadata URL is a web address that contains information designed to improve a webpage's search engine visibility and shareability on social platforms.

shows_in.description: Category channels that will be displayed to them

name.description: Translated category name

title.description: Translated Category SEO Metadata Title which is a concise label used to optimize search engine results and enhance the visibility of a category page.

description.description: A succinct summary crafted to enhance search engine optimization and spotlight a brand's attributes within a Translated category.

url.description: Translated Metadata URL is a web address that contains information designed to improve a webpage's search engine visibility and shareability on social platforms.

translations.description: Category translations are based on the store's enabled language locale. For instance, if the store supports both Arabic and English, the `translations` object will return two entries: `ar` for Arabic and `en` for English.

parent_id.description: A unique identifier that references the parent or higher-level category to which a specific category belongs, establishing a hierarchical relationship within a categorization system.

sort_order.description: Sort order dictates the sequence or arrangement of categories when displayed to users. It determines the order in which categories are presented, often using numerical values to assign priority, allowing for controlled and organized navigation.

id.description: Product ID. Get a list of Product IDs from [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394168)

sort.description: an Integer number signifying the sort order of the product inside the category

products.description: Assign products and specify the sort order.

scope_id.description: Passing scope Id will make the products sort is per scope in this category, multi country feature needs to be enabled.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: The Category ID. List of Category ID can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394207)

name.description: The title or label assigned to a specific grouping or classification of items, products, or content, often used to organize and categorize them for easier navigation or identification. 🌐 [Support multi-language](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421122)

status.description: The status of the category, whether or not it is `active` or `hidden`

metadata_title.description: Category SEO Metadata Title which is a concise label used to optimize search engine results and enhance the visibility of a category page.

metadata_description.description: A succinct summary crafted to enhance search engine optimization and spotlight a brand's attributes within a category.

metadata_url.description: Metadata URL is a web address that contains information designed to improve a webpage's search engine visibility and shareability on social platforms.

shows_in.description: Category channels that will be displayed to them

name.description: Translated category name

title.description: Translated Category SEO Metadata Title which is a concise label used to optimize search engine results and enhance the visibility of a category page.

description.description: A succinct summary crafted to enhance search engine optimization and spotlight a brand's attributes within a Translated category.

url.description: Translated Metadata URL is a web address that contains information designed to improve a webpage's search engine visibility and shareability on social platforms.

translations.description: Category translations are based on the store's enabled language locale. For instance, if the store supports both Arabic and English, the `translations` object will return two entries: `ar` for Arabic and `en` for English.

parent_id.description: A unique identifier that references the parent or higher-level category to which a specific category belongs, establishing a hierarchical relationship within a categorization system.

sort_order.description: Sort order dictates the sequence or arrangement of categories when displayed to users. It determines the order in which categories are presented, often using numerical values to assign priority, allowing for controlled and organized navigation.

id.description: Product ID. Get a list of Product IDs from [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394168)

sort.description: an Integer number signifying the sort order of the product inside the category

products.description: Assign products and specify the sort order.

scope_id.description: Passing scope Id will make the products sort is per scope in this category, multi country feature needs to be enabled.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a shipment related to your store directly from this endpoint.

:::danger[Breaking Change Notice]


<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Introducing National Address" icon="material-two-tone-signpost">
    **Effective January 5th, 2026**, the [National Address](https://splonline.com.sa/en/national-address-1/) details are **mandatory** with the `ship_to` object as follows:
    - `name`
    - `email`
    - `phone`
    - `country`
    - `city`
    - `address_line`
    - `street_number`
    - `block`
    - `short_address`
    - `building_number`
    - `additional_number`
    - `postal_code`
    - `geo_coordinates`
  </Card>

  <Card title="Ship Objects" icon="material-two-tone-local_shipping">
The following variables are deprecated. Update your Body request to use the
replacements below.

 | Object | Deprecated | Use Instead|
|------------|---------------------|--------------------------|
| `ship_to`    | `country_id`        | `ship_to.country`        |
| `ship_to`    | `city_id`           | `ship_to.city`           |
| `ship_from`  | `country_id`        | `ship_from.country`      |
| `ship_from`  | `country_code`      | `ship_from.country`      |
| `ship_from`  | `city_id`           | `ship_from.city`         |
  </Card>
</CardGroup>


:::


<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
  `shipping.read_write` – Shipping Read & Write
</Accordion>

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update specific shipment details by passing the `shipment_id` as a path parameter. 


:::danger[Breaking Change Notice]
Effective January 20th, 2025, the status variable will be a **required** field in the request payload.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`shipping.read_write`- Shipping Read & Write
</Accordion>

parameters.name: shipment_id
parameters.description: Shipment ID. Get a list of Shipment IDs from [here](https://docs.salla.dev/5394232e0)

status.description: Response status code, a numeric or alphanumeric identifier used to convey the outcome or status of a request, operation, or transaction in various systems and applications, typically indicating whether the action was successful, encountered an error, or resulted in a specific condition.

success.description: Response flag, boolean indicator used to signal a particular condition or state in the response of a system or application, often representing the presence or absence of certain conditions or outcomes.

id.description: A unique identifier for the shipment. Shipment list can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394232).

order_id.description: A unique identifier for the order associated with the shipment. List of orders can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394146)

order_reference_id.description: This field refers to a reference ID that can be used to look up additional information about the order

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the shipment provided by the external shipping company, used for cross-system reference.

external_additional_id.description: An alternate or supplementary identifier for the shipment, used for additional tracking or internal references.

created_at.description: Date and time of shipment creations.

type.description: Specifies the nature of the shipment, indicating whether it is an outgoing delivery to a customer ("shipment") or a return shipment sent back to the merchant ("return").

courier_id.description: Shipment courier identification. Find a complete list of Shipment companies [here](api-5578809/?nav=01HNA8MH78MVX1S0DRXDHE3A1K)

courier_name.description: The full name of the courier or shipping company responsible for transporting and delivering the shipment to its destination.

courier_logo.description: A URL pointing to the official logo image of the courier or shipping company, which can be used for display in user interfaces or documentation.

external_company_name.description: The name of the external shipping company used for shipments created via the API, if different from the standard courier list.

shipping_number.description: The unique shipping number assigned to the shipment by the courier, used for internal tracking and reference within the courier's system.

tracking_number.description: The unique tracking number provided by the courier, allowing customers and merchants to track the shipment's delivery status online.

pickup_id.description: A unique identifier for the shipment's pickup event, used to reference and manage the pickup process with the courier or logistics provider.

trackable.description: Indicates whether the shipment can be tracked online using a tracking number or link provided by the courier.

tracking_link.description: A direct URL to the courier's online tracking page for this shipment, allowing real-time status updates and location information.

format.description: The file format of the shipment label (e.g., PDF, PNG), which can be used for printing or digital reference.

url.description: A direct URL to download or view the shipment label file, which contains all necessary shipping and tracking information.

label.description: Detailed information about the shipment label, including its file format and a link to access the label.

payment_method.description: Specifies the payment method used for the shipment, such as cash on delivery (cod) or pre-paid, determining how the shipping cost is settled.

source.description: Indicates the origin of the shipment request, such as the dashboard, API, or other system sources.

status.description: Shipment Status

amount.description: The total monetary value of the shipment, representing the sum of all items and services included.

currency.description: The currency code (e.g., SAR, USD) in which the total shipment amount is denominated.

total.description: Details about the total value and currency of the shipment.

amount.description: The amount to be collected from the recipient upon delivery if the payment method is cash on delivery.

currency.description: The currency code (e.g., SAR, USD) for the cash on delivery amount.

cash_on_delivery.description: Details about the cash on delivery amount and its currency.

is_international.description: Indicates whether the shipment is being sent to a destination outside the origin country (international shipping).

value.description: The total weight of the shipment, including all packages, measured in the specified units.

units.description: The unit of measurement for the total weight (e.g., kg, g, lb, oz).

total_weight.description: Information about the total weight of the shipment and its measurement units.

billing_account.description: Indicates which billing account is used for the shipment charges, such as the merchant's own account or a platform account (e.g., Salla).

description.description: A brief summary or explanation of the contents or purpose of the shipment.

remarks.description: Any additional notes, comments, or special delivery instructions related to the shipment.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipping route.

name.description: The display name of the route assigned to the shipment.

shipping_route.description: The delivery route assigned to a shipping order

service_types.description: A list of service types requested for the shipment, Example: `domestic`,`international`,`normal`, `fulfillment`,`heavy`,`express`,`cash_on_delivery`,`cold`

item_id.description: A unique identifier for the item within the package, used for inventory and tracking purposes.

external_id.description: An external identifier for the item, which may be used by third-party systems or integrations.

name.description: The name or title of the item contained in the package.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code assigned to the item for inventory management.

amount.description: The price of a single unit of the item in the specified currency.

currency.description: The currency code for the item's price (e.g., SAR, USD).

quantity.description: The number of units of the item included in the package.

value.description: The weight of a single unit of the item, measured in the specified units.

units.description: The unit of measurement for the item's weight (e.g., kg, g, lb, oz).

name.description: A label describing a product variation or choice, such as size, color, or material.
The descriptive label or text representing a specific choice or value within a product option.

amount.description: The additional cost associated with this option value, if any.

currency.description: The currency code for the option value's price.

value.description: The actual value or selection for this product option.

values.description: An array of possible values for this product option, each with its own name, value, and price.

packages.description: A list of packages included in the shipment, each containing detailed information about the items, quantities, weights, and options.

type.description: Specifies the type of origin for the shipment, such as an address or branch location.

name.description: The name of the sender or origin contact person for the shipment.

email.description: The email address of the sender or origin contact.

phone.description: The phone number of the sender or origin contact.

branch_id.description: The unique identifier for the branch or facility from which the shipment is sent, if applicable.

country.description: The country from which the shipment is being sent.

city.description: The city from which the shipment is being sent.

id.description: Region identifier

name.description: Region name

code.description: Region code as defined by national standards.

region.description: Represents the geographic region details for the address.

address_line.description: The street address or location details for the shipment's origin.

street_number.description: The street number for the shipment's origin address, if applicable.

block.description: The block or building identifier for the shipment's origin address, if applicable.

short_address.description:
A compact 8-character Saudi address code (4 letters + 4 digits), e.g., RHMA3184.
It provides a simplified, precise version of the National Address to speed up delivery and reduce input errors.

building_number.description: The National Address building number associated with the location.

additional_number.description: An additional National Address identifier used for more precise location specification.

postal_code.description: The postal or ZIP code for the shipment's origin address, if applicable.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the shipment's origin location, used for mapping and routing.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the shipment's origin location, used for mapping and routing.

ship_from.description: Detailed information about the sender or origin location of the shipment, including contact details and address.

type.description: Specifies the type of destination for the shipment, such as an address or branch location.

name.description: The name of the recipient or destination contact person for the shipment.

email.description: The email address of the recipient or destination contact.

phone.description: The phone number of the recipient or destination contact.

country.description: The country to which the shipment is being sent.

city.description: The city to which the shipment is being sent.

id.description: Region identifier

name.description: Region name

code.description: Region code as defined by national standards.

region.description: Represents the geographic region details for the address.

address_line.description: The street address or location details for the shipment's destination.

street_number.description: The street number for the shipment's destination address.

block.description: The block or building identifier for the shipment's destination address.

short_address.description:
A compact 8-character Saudi address code (4 letters + 4 digits), e.g., RHMA3184.
It provides a simplified, precise version of the National Address to speed up delivery and reduce input errors.

building_number.description: The National Address building number associated with the location.

additional_number.description: An additional National Address identifier used for more precise location specification.

postal_code.description: The postal or ZIP code for the shipment's destination address.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the shipment's destination location, used for mapping and routing.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the shipment's destination location, used for mapping and routing.

ship_to.description: Detailed information about the recipient or destination location of the shipment, including contact details and address.

app_id.description: A unique identifier for the application or system that created or manages the shipment.

policy_options.description: Custom shipment policy details. Each key represents a policy option (e.g. number_of_boxes, shipment_content_type), and the value is a string. This allows flexibility for any store-defined policy.

customProperties.description: Each custom key here defines a store-level policy rule such as how many delivery attempts to make or the nature of the shipment contents. The values must be strings.

meta.description: Metadata providing additional context about the shipment, such as the originating application and policy options.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to fetch all shipping routes configured for the store.
Each route includes its type (e.g., normal, auto, branded, default), status, priority, and combination strategy.
These routes control how shipping options appear to customers at checkout.

:::warning[]
This endpoint is accessable only for allowed applications.
:::
<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">

</Accordion>

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new shipping route with configurable behavior, type, and conditions.
You can define the route type (normal, auto, or branded), control its priority and visibility, assign eligible shipping companies, and optionally add branded settings.
Conditions and strategies let you customize when and how the route should apply during checkout.

:::warning[]
This endpoint is accessable only for allowed applications.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`shipping.read_write` - Shipping Read & Write
</Accordion>

- type
- priority
- branded configuration
- assigned companies
- shipping conditions

This endpoint is accessable only for allowed applications.

This endpoint allows you to update the details of an existing shipping route by passing its `id` as a path parameter.
You can change the route name, type, status, and priority, and also update the list of companies, branded settings, conditions, and strategy.

This endpoint is accessable only for allowed applications.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create Single Coupon, and Group Coupons.

:::info[Information]
Although the response payload you will be receiving of the two coupon types at once, each has its own required Body Requests.
:::
<Card>

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Single Coupon">
                            
When creating a `Single Coupon`, ensure that you are sending the following required variables:

    | Attribute               | Type    | Is Required |
    | ----------------------- | ------- | ----------- |
    | `code`                  | string  | Yes         |
    | `type`                  | string  | Yes         |
    | `amount`                | number  | Yes         |
    | `free_shipping`         | boolean | Yes         |
    | `expiry_date`           | string  | Yes         |
    | `exclude_sale_products` | boolean | Yes         |

    If `marketing_active` is valued as `true`, then when creating `Marketing Coupons`, ensure that you are sending the
    following required variables:

    | Attribute          | Type    | Is Required |
    | ------------------ | ------- | ----------- |
    | `marketing_active` | boolean | Yes         |
    | `marketing_name`   | string  | Yes         |
    | `marketing_type`   | string  | Yes         |
    | `marketing_amount` | number  | Yes         |

  </Tab>
  
<Tab title="Group Coupons">
      
If `is_group` is valued as `true`, then when creating `Group Coupons`, ensure that you are sending the following required variables:

    | Attribute               | Type    | Is Required |
    | ----------------------- | ------- | ----------- |
    | `is_group`              | boolean | Yes         |
    | `group_coupons_count`   | number  | Yes         |
    | `group_suffix`          | string  | Yes         |
    | `group_name`            | string  | Yes         |
    | `type`                  | string  | Yes         |
    | `amount`                | number  | Yes         |
    | `free_shipping`         | boolean | Yes         |
    | `expiry_date`           | string  | Yes         |
    | `exclude_sale_products` | boolean | Yes         |

    If `marketing_active` is valued as `true`, then when creating `Marketing Coupons`, ensure that you are sending the following required variables:

    | Attribute          | Type    | Is Required |
    | ------------------ | ------- | ----------- |
    | `marketing_active` | boolean | Yes         |
    | `marketing_name`   | string  | Yes         |
    | `marketing_type`   | string  | Yes         |
    | `marketing_amount` | number  | Yes         |

</Tab>
</Tabs>
</Card>
<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
  `marketing.read_write`- Marketing Read & Write
</Accordion>

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to fetch User information based on the [Access Token](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421118) you have received, with a detailed response showcasing Store information.

:::tip
The `ID` , `email`, `name`, `mobile` and `role` are the information of the user who _authorized_ the app to be installed and it is __not__ always the store owner as it can be one of the store team.
:::

get.description:
An endpoint to generate tokens for various services for the current user:

1. [WebSocket connection token](https://github.com/SallaApp/Websocket#1-generate-the-connection-token)
2. [WebSocket subscription token](https://github.com/SallaApp/Websocket#2-private-channel-required-more-configuration-will-be-discussed-in-next-section)
3. [Reports service access token](https://2viqz69xcl.apidog.io/#Un0generating-the-token)


The min ttl for the tokens should be 48 hours


This endpoint shouldn't be accessible via any others token unless the hyper session token
This endpoint allows you to return the Store's detail information.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new branch and return the created branch id and its details.

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`branches.read_write`- Branchs Read & Write
</Accordion>

name.description: The distinctive title or label assigned to a specific location or division within an organization, typically used to differentiate and identify various operational units or physical establishments of that entity. 🌐 [Support multi-language](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421122)

city_id.description: Branch City ID, a unique identifier assigned to a specific city or urban area where a branch or location of an organization is situated, often used for reference and geographical categorization purposes. List of cities can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394230).

country_id.description:
Branch Country ID, a unique identifier assigned to a specific country where a branch or location of an organization is situated, typically used for reference and geographical categorization within organizational databases or systems.
List of countries can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394228).

location.description: Branch location on map ( longitude, latitude ),  the geographical coordinates that specify the precise position of a branch or location on the Earth's surface, with longitude indicating the east-west position and latitude indicating the north-south position.

cod_cost.description: The specific amount or fee associated with the payment method known as "cash on delivery," where customers pay for their orders in cash when they receive them, and this value represents any additional charge or fee related to this payment option.

is_cod_available.description: Whether or not Cash on delivery isavailable.

type.description: To distinguishes whether a specific location serves as a standard branch or a warehouse within an organization's infrastructure.

is_default.description: Whether or not this branch the default branch for all operations.

address_description.description: Branch address description,a textual explanation or details about the location and specifics of a branch within an organization, typically including information such as street address, building name, or additional identifying details. 🌐 [Support multi-language](doc-421122)

additional_number.description: Additional (alternative) phone number. Value length is 4 characters long.

building_number.description: Building Number. Value length is 4 characters long.

local.description: Represents the area that consists of all these buildings. (Neighborhood). 🌐 [Support multi-language](doc-421122)

postal_code.description: Branch postal code, the specific postal or ZIP code associated with a branch's location, aiding in the accurate sorting and delivery of mail and packages to that address.  Value length is 5 characters long

phone.description: Branch phone number, the designated telephone contact associated with a branch or location within an organization, facilitating communication with that specific site.

whatsapp.description: The designated WhatsApp contact associated with a branch or location within an organization, allowing for communication through the WhatsApp messaging platform.

telephone.description: Branch telephone number,  the designated phone contact associated with a branch or location within an organization, providing a means of communication through traditional telephone services.

preparation_time.description: The time required to get an order ready for shipping or picked up.

sunday.description: The day of the week when the branch is open. Values are: `sunday`, `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`

enabled.description: The option to enable the days as working day.

from.description: The beginning of the branch's working hours.

to.description: The conclusion of the branch's working hours.

working_hours.description: The hours where the branch is operating, it will be required if the `type` is branch.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to register a new webhook. 

:::tip[Note] 
- The webhook used is to notify/update/delete an external service when an event has occurred. 
- To trigger your webhook to send data, you can choose one event from the [List Events](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421119) endpoint. 
:::

:::info[Information]
Read more on Webhooks [here](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421119).
:::

:::caution[Alert]
• New subscriptions with the same URL will update events / restore old webhooks *(if they exist)*.
• The added URL **must** accept `POST` requests.
• By default, all new webhooks are registered as version `2`. To use version `1`, specify it in your request parameters.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`webhooks.read_write`- Webhooks Read & Write
</Accordion>

error.message: The access token should have access to one of those scopes: webhooks.read_write

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update an existing webhook by passing the `id` as path parameter


:::tip[Note] 
- The webhook used is to notify/update/delete an external service when an event has occurred. 
- To trigger your webhook to send data, you can choose one event from the [List Events](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421119) endpoint. 
:::

:::info[Information]
Read more on Webhooks [here](https://docs.salla.dev/doc-421119).
:::

:::caution[Alert]
• New subscriptions with the same URL will update events / restore old webhooks *(if they exist)*.
• The added URL **must** accept `PUT` requests.
• By default, all new webhooks are registered as version `2`. To use version `1`, specify it in your request parameters.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`webhooks.read_write`- Webhooks Read & Write
</Accordion>

delete.description:
This endpoint allows you to unsubscribe/deactivate a webhook from the active webhooks list at your store. 

:::tip[Note]

- You can define which webhook to delete by using `url` and/or `id` in the query parameters.

:::

:::caution[Alert]
- Using `url` will **delete** all registered webhooks to this URL.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`webhooks.read_write`- Webhooks Read & Write
</Accordion>

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Webhook ID. `requiredif` no URL passed. List of Webhook IDs can be found [here](https://docs.salla.dev/api-5394135).

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Webhook registered URL. `requiredif` no ID is passed.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to list product review, general product, and shipping ratings as well as Merchant store reviews.

:::info[Review Types]
- Product reviews are of type `rating`.
- General product questions are of type `ask`.
- Ratings about shipping are of type `shipping`.
- Merchant store reviews are of type `testimonial`.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`reviews.read`- Reviews Read Only
</Accordion>

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: The content of feedback

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of feedback

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date filter in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date filter in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

parameters.name: products
parameters.description: List of product IDs to filter feedbacks.

parameters.name: blogs
parameters.description: List of blog IDs to filter feedbacks.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to list product feedbacks, general product, blog,and shipping ratings as well as Merchant store feedbacks.

:::info[Feedback Types]
- Product reviews are of type ```product```. 
- General product questions are of type ```ask```.
- Ratings about shipping are of type ```shipping```.
- Merchant store feedbacks are of type ```store```.
- Merchant blog feedbacks are of type ```blog```.
- Merchant reported feedbacks are of type ```reported```.
:::

<Accordion title="Scopes" defaultOpen={true} icon="lucide-key-round">
`reviews.read`- Questions & Reviews Read Only
</Accordion>

This endpoint allows you to upload a file to our CDN based on its use.

:::check[]
For example, when you need to attach a receipt image to an order, you must first upload the image. After uploading, you can use the URL to attach it to the order.
:::

type.description: File type

status.description: n various systems and applications, typically indicating whether the action was successful, encountered an error, or resulted in a specific condition.

success.description: Response flag, boolean indicator used to signal a particular condition or state in the response of a system or application, often representing the presence or absence of certain conditions or outcomes.

url.description: File's uploaded URL

post.description:
Uploads files and images to Cloudflare R2 storage with automatic image optimization.

**Accepted file types:**
- Images: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, AVIF
- Documents: PDF, DOC, DOCX, CSV, TXT
- Spreadsheets: XLS, XLSX
- Presentations: PPTX
- Audio: MP3, WAV, OGG

**Image Processing:**
- **All images are automatically resized and optimized** with defaults (1920x1080, JPEG, 90% quality)
- Width and height parameters act as **maximum dimensions** - images are scaled to fit within these bounds while **preserving aspect ratio**
- If only width is specified, height is calculated to maintain aspect ratio
- If only height is specified, width is calculated to maintain aspect ratio
- If both are specified, the image fits within the bounding box (whichever dimension is limiting)
- **Example**: A 3840×2160 image with width=1920, height=1080 becomes 1920×1080 (16:9 ratio maintained)
- **Example**: A 2000×1000 image with width=1920, height=1080 becomes 1920×960 (2:1 ratio maintained)
- Resized images must be under 512KB

**File Processing:**
- Non-image files are uploaded directly without processing

**Storage & Access:**
- Maximum upload size: 100MB
- Files in `digitals` category are private (no cache)
- All other files are public with CDN caching (max-age: 1 year)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: File/Image category for organization and access control

file.description: File or image to upload (max 100MB). This is the only required field.

width.description: Maximum width in pixels (images only). Images are scaled down to fit within this width while preserving aspect ratio. If only width is provided, height is calculated automatically. If both width and height are provided, the image fits within the bounding box.

height.description: Maximum height in pixels (images only). Images are scaled down to fit within this height while preserving aspect ratio. If only height is provided, width is calculated automatically. If both width and height are provided, the image fits within the bounding box.

quality.description: Output image quality 1-100 (images only). Higher values produce better quality but larger file sizes.

format.description: Output image format (images only). JPEG is default. WebP and AVIF offer better compression. PNG for transparency.

2.summary: Image with default settings (1920x1080, JPEG, 90% quality)

3.summary: Image with custom width (800px), aspect ratio preserved

data.message: Content-Type must be multipart/form-data

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

data.message: Unauthorized
Invalid type. Allowed values: orders, payments, checkout, cart, products, brands, categories, digitals, customers, homepage, theme, meta
File size exceeds maximum allowed size of 100MB
Resized image size exceeds maximum allowed size of 512KB

500.description: Internal server error - Image processing failed

data.message: Failed to process image

id.description: A unique alphanumeric code or identifier assigned to a specific order.

reference_id.description: A specific alphanumeric code or identifier associated with an order.

total.description: The amount representing the total expense incurred for all the items in the order.

status.description: Status of the order.

draft.description: Whether or not the order is a draft or no.

read.description: Whether or not the order is read by the merchant or not

can_cancel.description:
Whether or not the order is allowed to be cancelled by store customers.
`True` value should be set if the order status is in under review and in progress, as according to the store settings.

can_reorder.description: Whether or not the order allowed to be re-order by customers.

is_pending_payment.description: Whether or not the order is waiting to be paid or the order status is `payment_pending`.

pending_payment_ends_at.description: The time untill the payment has to be made by the cusotmer

digitalable.description: Whether or not there is a digital label for the order

shippable.description: Whether or not this order is shippable by shipping companies

has_suspicious_alert.description: Whether or not the order contains any suspicious fraud payments

name.description: The label or description associated with a specific item.

quantity.description: The numerical value representing the number of units or items of a specific product.

id.description: A unique identifier for the customer.

mobile.description: The customer's mobile phone number without the country code.

mobile_code.description: The country code for the customer's mobile phone number.

email.description: The customer's email address.

urls.description: A list of URLs associated with the customer, such as their website or social media profiles.

avatar.description: A URL to the customer's avatar image.

timezone_type.description: The time zone type for the customer's date of birth.

timezone.description: The time zone for the customer's date of birth.

city.description: The city where the customer lives.

country.description: The country where the customer lives.

country_code.description: The country code for the customer's country.

currency.description: The currency that the customer uses.

location.description: The customer's location, represented as a string.

updated_at.description: The date and time when the customer's information was last updated.

groups.description: A list of group IDs that the customer belongs to.

data.description: Detailed structure of the List Order model object showing its fields and data types.

pagination.description: For a better response behavior as well as maintain the best security level, All retrieving API endpoints use a mechanism to retrieve data in chunks called pagination. Pagination working by return only a specific number of records in each response, and through passing the page number you can navigate the different pages.
